audit_status.source_base: Greek Gospel text tradition, draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: Compact complete draft reviewed for lost sheep, lost coin, and lost sons/father parable with table fellowship frame and joy over turning/realignment.

verses.restored: All the tax collectors and sinners/misaligned ones were drawing near to hear him.
The Pharisees and scribes were grumbling, saying, This one receives sinners/misaligned ones and eats with them.
He spoke this parable to them, saying,
What person among you, having a hundred sheep and losing one of them, does not leave the ninety-nine in the wilderness and go after the lost one until he finds it?
Finding it, he lays it on his shoulders, rejoicing.
Coming into the house, he calls together friends and neighbors, saying to them, Rejoice with me, because I found my sheep that was lost.
I say to you: likewise there will be joy in heaven over one sinner/misaligned one turning/realigning, more than over ninety-nine righteous ones who have no need of turning/realignment.
Or what woman having ten drachmas, if she loses one drachma, does not light a lamp, sweep the house, and seek carefully until she finds it?
Finding it, she calls together friends and neighbors, saying, Rejoice with me, because I found the drachma that I lost.
Likewise, I say to you, there is joy before the messengers of the Divine over one sinner/misaligned one turning/realigning.
He said, A certain person had two sons.
The younger of them said to the father, Father, give me the share of the property falling to me. He divided the living between them.
Not many days later, gathering everything, the younger son traveled to a far country, and there scattered his property, living wastefully.
When he had spent everything, a severe famine came through that country, and he began to be in need.
He went and joined himself to one of the citizens of that country, who sent him into his fields to feed pigs.
He longed to be filled from the pods that the pigs were eating, and no one was giving anything to him.
Coming to himself, he said, How many hired workers of my father have abundance of bread, but I perish here with hunger.
Rising, I will go to my father and say to him, Father, I sinned/misaligned against heaven and before you.
I am no longer worthy to be called your son; make me as one of your hired workers.
Rising, he came to his father. While he was still far away, his father saw him and was moved with compassion; running, he fell on his neck and kissed him fervently.
The son said to him, Father, I sinned/misaligned against heaven and before you; I am no longer worthy to be called your son.
But the father said to his servants, Quickly bring out the first robe and clothe him; give a ring to his hand and sandals to his feet.
Bring the fattened calf, slaughter it, and let us eat and rejoice.
Because this son of mine was dead and came to life again; he was lost and was found. And they began to rejoice.
Now his older son was in the field. As he came and drew near to the house, he heard music and dancing.
Calling one of the servants, he asked what these things might be.
He said to him, Your brother has come, and your father slaughtered the fattened calf because he received him back healthy.
He became angry and was not willing to enter. His father came out and pleaded with him.
He answered his father, Look, so many years I serve you, and I never transgressed your command, yet you never gave me a young goat so I might rejoice with my friends.
But when this son of yours came, the one devouring your living with prostitutes, you slaughtered the fattened calf for him.
He said to him, Child, you are always with me, and all that is mine is yours.
But it was necessary to rejoice and be glad, because this brother of yours was dead and came to life; he was lost and was found.
